steps.title: Relate q to t
steps.content: The original model uses $t$ in years. Since there are 4 quarters per year, $q$ quarters equals $t = \frac{q}{4}$ years.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $M = 1800 \times (1.02)^{q/4}$ — matches the substitution since $t = q/4$. Correct.
  • B) $M = 1800 \times (1.02)^{4q}$ — would mean $t = 4q$, treating each quarter as 4 years. Wrong.
  • C) $M = 1800 \times (1.005)^{4q}$ — uses a different base 1.005 with exponent $4q$, giving $(1.005)^{4} \approx 1.0201$ per quarter cycle of 4, not the correct per-quarter growth. Wrong.
  • D) $M = 1800 \times (1.082)^{q}$ — treats each quarter as a full year of growth at rate 8.2%, which contradicts the 2% annual rate. Wrong.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
